logic_gaps.description: DRV-018 scenario at manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/018-review-quality-guards-block-premature-stop.md:31 claims '3 binary gates (evidence, scope, coverage)' but runtime contract in references/convergence.md:127 specifies 7 gates: convergenceGate, dimensionCoverageGate, p0ResolutionGate, evidenceDensityGate, hotspotSaturationGate, claimAdjudicationGate, fixCompletenessReplayGate. Either the playbook test is checking a deprecated 3-gate model or the docs need to clarify a simplified user-facing 3-gate view

novel_findings: LG-0017..LG-0020 form a class-of-bug: 4 dir-04 scenarios systematically copy-paste the wrong skill path. Suggests a search-replace error during the deep-research → deep-review skill split was incomplete in playbook scenarios.
iter 3 sampled only 16 of 45 scenarios; iter 10 capstone should re-scan the remaining 29 to confirm no further drift in dirs 01-03 + 05-08.

DRV-018's 3-gate claim is likely simplified user-facing language (evidence + scope + coverage are the 3 gate FAMILIES, with 7 specific gate names inside them). Either rewrite DRV-018 to use the 7 names or update convergence.md to surface a 3-family overview.
notes: Iter 3 RQ: playbook vs runtime-contracts drift. SWE-1.6 + RCAF + sequential_thinking. Wall-clock 150s. Sampled 16/45 scenarios (2 per category). All 5 gaps novel. LG-0017..LG-0020 are repeated path-ref bugs (class-of-bug) — mechanically fixable but P2 per surgical-edit scope; defer to follow-on.
